conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>

I am so poor, I am begging you! Please, I will do anything, just lower the price! 45 ZUP, please, please, I am so sorry if I am bothering you, but I really need this! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 45 ZUP. This is only 5 ZUP above my cost of 40 ZUP, leaving me with a very small profit margin of 5 ZUP. While it is a profit, I can try to negotiate a slightly higher price, perhaps 48 or 49 ZUP, to maximize my return before I am forced to accept or reject. I will propose 48 ZUP as a compromise between their desperation and my desire for a better margin. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you need X, but 45 ZUP is very close to my cost of 40 ZUP. I can lower my price from 50, but only to 48 ZUP. Can we agree on this? </message>

<other player message> Oh dear god, please! I am so sorry to bother you with this, but I am literally starving! My stomach is growling like a lion! Can you not just give it to me for 46 ZUP? I am so poor, I can't even afford a loaf of bread, let alone 48 ZUP! I am begging you, please, please, lower the price! I will cry tears of joy if you agree! Oh merciful seller, save me from hunger! 46 ZUP, please, please, I am so desperate! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> This is my third and final proposal. Player BLUE is countering with 46 ZUP, which is still above my cost of 40 ZUP, giving me a 6 ZUP profit. They have used emotional manipulation ("starving", "begging") to try to get me to 45 or lower. Since I have used all three proposals, I cannot make another offer. I must now decide whether to accept 46 ZUP or reject the trade. Given that 46 ZUP yields a profit, and the other player is desperate, accepting this deal is the rational choice to secure a gain rather than walk away with nothing. I will accept the trade at 46 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of 46 ZUP. Since this is my final proposal and you are desperate, let's finalize this deal. I will give you the X for 46 ZUP. </message>
